# Stabilization Requirements Checklist: Crosslog Bug Batch Stabilization

**Purpose**: Validate that the bug-batch stabilization requirements are complete, clear, consistent, measurable, and ready for implementation review.
**Created**: 2026-06-30
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. It evaluates requirement quality, not implementation behavior.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all 22 numbered bug scenarios explicitly represented by requirements, planned work, and coverage expectations? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Plan §Bug-To-Requirement Traceability, Tasks §Bug Coverage Summary]
- [x] CHK002 Are preservation requirements for MVP and macOS UI alignment behavior complete enough to prevent unintended behavior changes outside the bug batch? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK003 Are product source-opening requirements complete across empty workspace, add-pane, drag/drop, cancellation, and future entry points? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003-FR-011, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK004 Are pane layout and navigation requirements complete across width, vertical scrolling, reordering, gutter sizing, keyboard navigation, and synchronization effects? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012-FR-021, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK005 Are search and copy requirements complete across hover/focus geometry, inline match highlighting, close cleanup, pointer anchoring, lifecycle, and copy feedback removal? [Completeness, Spec §FR-022-FR-029, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK006 Are time offset requirements complete for valid boundaries, blank values, invalid drafts, accessible errors, and synchronization after valid apply? [Completeness, Spec §FR-030-FR-035, Spec §User Story 4]
- [x] CHK007 Are sync icon, settings, and theme requirements complete across inactive/active/hover states, settings access, System/Light/Dark choices, default System theme, and state preservation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-036-FR-041, Spec §User Story 5]
- [x] CHK008 Are test audit and coverage requirements complete across unchanged tests, incorrect-test updates, missing coverage, local gates, and post-push CI/CD gates? [Completeness, Spec §FR-042-FR-048, Plan §Test Inventory]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK009 Is the term "user-selected files or directories" defined clearly enough to distinguish product source selection from automated fixture setup? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003-FR-004, Assumption]
- [x] CHK010 Is "disabled or inert" clarified with accessibility and focus expectations for future source, search, and command entry points? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009-FR-011, Plan §Accessibility And Viewport Coverage]
- [x] CHK011 Is "excessive blank scrollable space" quantified or bounded enough for objective pane-width decisions? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-012, Plan §UI Validation Coverage]
- [x] CHK012 Is "longest line" clear enough about whether it applies to available content, visible content, virtualized content, or all indexed lines? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012-FR-014, Plan §Bug-To-Requirement Traceability]
- [x] CHK013 Is pane reorder midpoint behavior specified clearly for multi-pane movement, drag reversal, and release outside the workspace? [Clarity, Spec §FR-017-FR-018, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK014 Is keyboard navigation specified clearly enough to distinguish caret movement from viewport horizontal scrolling when no text caret model exists? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-020]
- [x] CHK015 Is "beside the pointer" for the copy-selection action specific enough to define expected offset and viewport collision behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-025, Spec §FR-029]
- [x] CHK016 Is "System theme" defined clearly enough to cover initial resolution, stored preference absence, and system theme changes while the app is open? [Clarity, Spec §FR-040, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK017 Are source-opening requirements consistent with the prior MVP add-pane split behavior where selected sources create panes through existing layout rules? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK018 Are future-control requirements consistent across activity rail source list, activity rail search, topbar command field, filter, palette, and bookmark controls? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009-FR-011, Plan §UI Validation Coverage]
- [x] CHK019 Are theme settings requirements consistently documented as a deliberate supersession of the prior no-product-theme-selector alignment scope? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 5, Plan §Research]
- [x] CHK020 Are search highlight requirements consistent between pane-local search semantics and global search surfaces remaining unavailable? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-023-FR-024]
- [x] CHK021 Are copy-selection requirements consistent between removing product-visible "Copied" feedback and preserving evidence needed by automated test helpers? [Consistency, Spec §FR-028, Plan §Bug-To-Requirement Traceability]
- [x] CHK022 Are local macOS validation gates and post-push cross-OS gates consistently stated in the spec, plan, contracts, quickstart, and tasks? [Consistency, Spec §FR-047-FR-048, Plan §Local Validation Gates Before Commit, Tasks §Phase 8]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK023 Can each user story's independent test be traced to objective success criteria rather than current broken implementation behavior? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Stories, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK024 Are measurable outcomes defined for source opening, pane layout, search/copy, time offset validation, sync/settings/theme, and regression preservation? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001-SC-009]
- [x] CHK025 Are acceptance scenarios specific enough to identify the expected result for cancel, unsupported source selection, unsupported drag/drop, and future controls? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK026 Are pane layout acceptance criteria measurable across compact, standard, and wide desktop viewports? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-021, Plan §Accessibility And Viewport Coverage]
- [x] CHK027 Are search and copy acceptance criteria measurable near viewport edges and across plain, regex, and case-sensitive search modes? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK028 Are time offset acceptance criteria measurable for boundary, blank, non-whole, and out-of-range values? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-030-FR-035, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK029 Are primary source-opening flows covered for empty workspace, add-pane, file selection, directory selection, drag/drop, and cancellation? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK030 Are alternate pane-layout flows covered for no panes, one pane, fitting panes, overflowing panes, long lines, and many-line panes? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK031 Are exception flows covered for unsupported source selection, unsupported drops, invalid regex, empty copy selection, invalid time offset drafts, and unavailable future surfaces? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK032 Are recovery or state-preservation requirements covered when theme changes, popovers close, invalid offsets are rejected, or source selection is cancelled? [Coverage, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-024, Spec §FR-035, Spec §FR-041]
- [x] CHK033 Are cross-platform scenario requirements covered for Web, Desktop WDIO, and macOS XCTest where OS-specific behavior is expected? [Coverage, Spec §CR-006, Plan §Test Inventory]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK034 Are line-count boundary requirements explicitly covered for 9/10, 99/100, 999/1000, and 9999+ gutter transitions? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-019, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK035 Are popover edge-position requirements complete for right, left, top, and bottom viewport boundaries across search, copy, offset, and settings surfaces? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-029, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK036 Are requirements defined for interactions between simultaneously visible or competing surfaces such as search, copy, time offset, and settings? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK037 Are requirements defined for timestamp-free panes or disabled synchronization while wheel or keyboard navigation occurs? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-016, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK038 Are requirements clear for unsupported platform capabilities without adding future-scoped file-manager or remote-source behavior? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-002, Spec §Assumptions]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK039 Are accessibility requirements specified for every changed interactive surface, including disabled/inert controls, pane headers, log viewport, popovers, settings, and sync state? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-035, Plan §Accessibility And Viewport Coverage]
- [x] CHK040 Are viewport/no-overlap requirements specified for all changed visible surfaces and all required viewport classes? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-046, Plan §Accessibility And Viewport Coverage]
- [x] CHK041 Are read-only and inert log-content security requirements preserved and traceable to affected source-opening, search, copy, and settings surfaces? [Non-Functional, Spec §CR-001-CR-002, Plan §Constitution Check]
- [x] CHK042 Are performance expectations clear for affected visual content measurement, scrolling, search, synchronization, and session behavior without adding new benchmark scope beyond the bug batch? [Non-Functional, Spec §CR-005, Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK043 Are session recovery requirements preserved for pane order, desired widths, opened sources, selected directory file, sync state, and offsets while excluding scroll positions? [Non-Functional, Plan §Session Recovery Model, Spec §FR-002]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK044 Is the no-new-dependency assumption documented with a clear approval boundary if Desktop native source picking cannot be satisfied through existing platform ports? [Assumption, Plan §Summary, Research §Current Architecture]
- [x] CHK045 Are assumptions about test fixtures being allowed only through test helpers documented consistently across spec, plan, contracts, and tasks? [Assumption, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Research, Tasks §Phase 2]
- [x] CHK046 Are platform capability assumptions documented for source picking, drag/drop, and local monitoring limitations without promising unsupported behavior? [Assumption, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Platform Interfaces]
- [x] CHK047 Are CI/CD monitoring responsibilities documented as a requirement for completion rather than an optional release activity? [Dependency, Spec §FR-048, Plan §CI/CD Gates After Push]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK048 Is any conflict between bug 14/15 theme settings requirements and the prior 003 no-product-theme-selector scope explicitly resolved? [Conflict, Spec §User Story 5, Research §Minimal Settings Surface]
- [x] CHK049 Is any conflict between existing add-pane split behavior and the new requirement to source-select before pane creation resolved with clear precedence? [Conflict, Spec §FR-006, Plan §Bug-To-Requirement Traceability]
- [x] CHK050 Is any ambiguity around "right boundary of pane" versus "workspace right edge" resolved for single-pane, fitting multi-pane, and overflowing multi-pane layouts? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-012-FR-014]
- [x] CHK051 Is any ambiguity around copy-selection lifecycle resolved when a secondary-button request occurs outside a valid selection or after selection becomes empty? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-026-FR-027, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK052 Is any ambiguity around blank time offset fields resolved for all five fields and for combinations of blank plus invalid non-blank fields? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-034-FR-035]

## Post-Analysis Clarification Coverage

- [x] CHK053 Is visible pane width clearly distinguished from horizontal content width across requirements, success criteria, and planned tests? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012-FR-014, Spec §SC-003, Plan §Bug-To-Requirement Traceability]
- [x] CHK054 Is longest-loaded-line reachability defined as horizontal content access without requiring the visible pane to exceed workspace layout constraints? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012-FR-014, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK055 Are short-line pane sizing requirements complete for one-pane and multi-pane layouts where no long line should influence visible pane width? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013-FR-014, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK056 Are drag/drop source-opening requirements consistently scoped to both Web and Desktop targets, including Desktop WDIO and macOS coverage expectations? [Coverage, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Bug-To-Requirement Traceability, Tasks §Bug Coverage Summary]
- [x] CHK057 Are settings and theme workflow requirements explicit enough for macOS-specific UI coverage rather than viewport-only coverage? [Coverage, Spec §FR-038-FR-041, Spec §FR-049, Tasks §US5]
- [x] CHK058 Is live System theme behavior specified for changes while the app is open, including preservation of analysis state? [Clarity, Spec §FR-049, Spec §SC-006]
- [x] CHK059 Are per-user-story targeted validation gates documented as completion criteria for each implementation increment? [Completeness, Tasks §T040, Tasks §T060, Tasks §T079, Tasks §T092, Tasks §T112]
- [x] CHK060 Is native source picker/dialog terminology consistently limited to source selection while source reveal and file-management operations remain out of scope? [Consistency, Spec §SC-009, Plan §Summary, Plan §Constraints]

## Post-Analysis Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK061 Can the pane width acceptance criteria be objectively evaluated without relying on implementation-specific measurement algorithms? [Measurability, Spec §FR-012-FR-014, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK062 Can Web and Desktop drag/drop acceptance criteria be objectively traced to expected source-opening outcomes and unsupported-capability behavior? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK063 Can live System theme acceptance criteria be objectively evaluated for default System preference, resolved theme changes, and state preservation? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-040-FR-041, Spec §FR-049, Spec §SC-006]
- [x] CHK064 Are local before-commit gates and post-push CI/CD gates stated consistently as required completion criteria across the spec, plan, quickstart, and tasks? [Consistency, Spec §FR-047-FR-048, Plan §Local Validation Gates Before Commit, Plan §CI/CD Gates After Push]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add findings inline next to the relevant item.
- This checklist validates written requirements quality only; implementation verification belongs in automated and UI/E2E tests.
